recommended_paths: Complete the required audit-floor reviews, write the RR-AUTH-04 acceptance ledger, and attach the Claude comparison plus guard reruns before any closure claim.
exact_next_step: Record reviewer resolutions in the governing TODO using the machine-checkable resolution table or equivalent gate ledger, then extract the derived resolution packet and decide whether another bounded review pass is still required.

findings.title: Required audit-floor gates are still open
findings.rationale: The TODO and bounded package still marked critique, security, verification-debt, test-quality, final-review, triple-audit, and Claude comparison as unresolved. That means the current implementation evidence cannot be promoted to closure authority yet.
findings.suggested_action: Do not mark RR-AUTH-04 or the auth tranche as passed until the required review lanes, triple audit, Claude comparison, and guard reruns are actually recorded.

findings.title: No durable RR-AUTH-04 acceptance ledger exists yet
findings.rationale: The bounded slice has focused validation and review dispatch artifacts, but it still lacks one durable ledger that normalizes what was found, what was fixed, and what remains accepted debt versus blocker. That leaves the acceptance decision too dependent on chat chronology.
findings.suggested_action: Write an RR-AUTH-04 audit-floor acceptance ledger that records lane outcomes, accepted debt, validation evidence, and the exact next gate.

findings.title: Closure claims outrun independent corroboration
findings.rationale: The current artifact set already describes the implementation as complete, but the no-context review and triple-audit artifacts were not yet reconciled into that claim. Without those independent outputs, the authority packet still overstates closure readiness.
findings.suggested_action: Keep the TODO/package language explicitly at reconciliation-in-progress until the independent review lanes are merged and any remaining debt is formally accepted.
